channel.title: RSS Aggregator Feed
channel.description: An aggregated feed of Technology-related news

item.title: Apple, Samsung, and Google phones are all on sale for Memorial Day — these are the 12 best deals from Amazon, Verizon, T-Mobile, and others today
item.description: As someone who's covered cell phone deals for years, these are the 12 best Memorial Day promotions that I'd personally recommend from Verizon, T-Mobile, Amazon, and other retailers.

item.title: Samsung's Memorial Day TV sale ends today — here are 15 deals worth buying, including up to $1,500 off top-rated 4K, QLED, and OLED TVs
item.description: Samsung's Memorial Day TV sale ends tonight, and I've rounded up the best deals worth buying with up to $1,500 in savings on Samsung's top-rated 4K, QLED, and OLED TVs.

item.title: 'This technology turns every router into a potential means for surveillance': researchers warn you can be tracked and identified from Wi-Fi signals
item.description: Feedback signals used by ordinary routers can be tapped into to identify you based on the way you walk.

item.title: 12 of the best Memorial Day laptop deals — my top picks from Dell, Best Buy, Apple, HP, and Lenovo from $179
item.description: I've searched through all of this year's Memorial Day laptop sales and picked out the top 12 offers I'd recommend, with prices starting as low as $179.

item.title: Pope Leo calls for AI to serve humanity and not concentrate power
item.description: In 42,300 word encyclical, Pope Leo warns of AI dangers around educational, child safety, concentration of power and weapons.

item.title: Flambée de l’énergie : les Européens se ruent sur les panneaux solaires et ça ne va pas s’arrêter
item.description: La crise énergétique fait exploser les ventes de panneaux solaires en Europe. Certains acteurs voient leurs ventes décupler. Pendant ce temps, les aides publiques fondent.

item.title: ‘An impressive phone that punches above its price’ — I tested the Honor 600 and was blown away by its blazing display, epic battery life, and occasionally uncanny AI
item.description: I tested Honor’s AI-packed mid-range phone and was impressed (and occasionally unnerved) by its powerful features.

item.title: Test de l’Anker MagGo Power Bank 10K Slim : la batterie sans fil compacte pensée pour les produits Apple
item.description: Anker a su se construire une réputation de fiabilité sur ses produits dédiés à la recharge. Avec cette MagGo Power Bank 10 K est ce que la marque transforme l'essai pour les iPhone et leur recharge MagSafe ? Nous l'avons testée et voici notre verdict.

item.title: Les Pays-Bas construisent leur propre GitHub pour ne plus dépendre de Microsoft
item.description: L'État néerlandais héberge désormais son code sur une plateforme maison, 100 % libre. La France a le même problème, mais pas la même solution.

item.title: 'After one month, most partners have each found hundreds of critical- or high-severity vulnerabilities': Anthropic claims Mythos has found over ten thousand major security vulnerabilities across 'the most systemically important software in the world'
item.description: Anthropic illustrates the first months of Mythos Preview, saying it discovered thousands of critical and high-severity bugs.

item.title: These Privacy-Conscious Gay Dating Apps Want to Dethrone Grindr
item.description: As public backlash against Big Dating mounts, a batch of new gay hookup apps offer community-powered alternatives.

item.title: La JBL Partybox 130 s’invite à la fête : cette nouvelle enceinte de soirée avec un son 200 W RMS affiche déjà -50 €
item.description: L'enceinte sono JBL Partybox 130 est un modèle à usage festif qui propose 15h d'autonomie, qui se retrouve en promo à 349,46 euros sur Electro Depot au lieu de 399,99 euros.

item.title: Zendesk CLO Shana Simmons: Empathy is the new superpower for AI leaders
item.description: Zendesk CLO Shana Simmons says AI success hinges on a privacy-first culture and governance. Empathy, character, and agency are the traits we’ll need in an AI-first world.

item.title: In Defense of My Attachment to This Lululemon Duffel Bag (2026)
item.description: Say what you will about the brand's leggings, but you'll never convince me to hate this marvel of a carryall.

item.title: Use Tiny11 to Rescue a Computer Running Windows 10
item.description: If you can’t—or don’t want to—upgrade to full Windows 11, consider this lightweight version of Microsoft’s operating system that works on a wide range of computers.

item.title: 5 things Microsoft isn't fixing with Windows 11 that I'd love to see happen
item.description: If Microsoft is serious about crowd-pleasing changes — and that appears to be the case — here's how it could score some huge wins with everyday users.

item.title: CEOs and other leaders share their summer book picks
item.description: Here’s what CEOs will be reading on the beach. Plus: another way CEOs can build trust in AI. Hello and welcome to Modern CEO! I’m Stephanie Mehta, CEO and chief content officer of Mansueto Ventures. Each week this newsletter explores inclusive approaches to leadership drawn from conversations with executives and entrepreneurs, and from the pages of Inc. and Fast Company. If you received this newsletter from a friend, you can sign up to get it yourself every Monday morning. ...

item.title: Wendy’s is holding a look-alike contest—with a grand prize of free burgers for a year—to celebrate World Redhead Day
item.description: The fast-food chain will award the person who can showcase the best version of its iconic logo. Calling all redheads to the front! Wendy’s is honoring its roots by holding a look-alike contest for its iconic logo in New York City on May 26, World Redhead Day.

item.title: Ubisoft s’appuierait bien sur l’IA générative pour Far Cry 7, mais le pire nous serait épargné
item.description: Ubisoft investit massivement dans l'intelligence artificielle pour accélérer ou étoffer le développement de certains jeux. On apprenait il y a quelques jours que la firme en ferait notamment usage pour Far Cry 7... mais uniquement en R&D.

item.title: Why fiber is the real secret to scaling intelligence in artificial intelligence factories
item.description: AI factories can’t scale on silicon alone; dense fiber is the real intelligence layer.

item.title: Most ransomware attacks are opportunistic. Here’s how you can stop attackers
item.description: Ransomware isn’t targeted - it exploits predictable gaps like poor hygiene and missing basics.

item.title: How ‘Shift Work Sleep Disorder’ is hurting workers—and costing employers
item.description: Shift Work Sleep Disorder (SWSD) affects up to 40% of U.S. shift workers, Luckily, it’s treatable. Before most of America pours its first cup of coffee, millions of workers are already hours into their shifts, and they’re tired. Overnight warehouse workers are packing orders. Early-morning bus drivers are taking kids to school. ER nurses are handing off to the day team at 7 a.m. These workers aren’t just fatigued from long hours. Many are living with a real, diagnosable medical condition that goes unrecognized and untreated, sometimes for years. And the cost of that gap doesn’t stay invisible ...
